name: Retune the accepted B-first cp.async handoff inside the K16 hot band

summary.name: Retune the accepted B-first cp.async handoff inside the K16 hot band
summary.hypothesis: The accepted round-52/53 base is still the right surface: grouped-row=8, K16, no B lookahead, single-scratch export, and B-first refill ordering. The latest run only regressed by 0.009152 ms, so the remaining gap looks like a very narrow steady-state handoff issue between cp.async issue, wait/commit timing, and the first PTX B-fragment consume order rather than a new memory-balance problem. Keep the accepted base intact and tighten only the B-first feed/issue handoff.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Async-copy feed/issue retiming and stage handoff latency inside the active PTX hot-band microkernel.
summary.risk: Low to moderate. The upside is the best in this round, but it is easy to slide back into a disguised unroll-1 or lookahead-style rewrite if the handoff is changed too broadly.
metrics_to_recheck: median runtime versus 24.895488 ms accepted base
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the change does not beat the 24.895488 ms accepted base, or if it starts behaving like another K32 or unroll-1 branch.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
